title: Physics Foundations
description: Learn and master the concepts of Physics Foundations with structured lessons, interactive visualizations, and practice exercises.

title: Kinematics
description: This course prepares students for **Course 2: Kinematics**, where they'll begin studying motion mathematically. It ensures everyone has the measurement, vector, and mathematical foundations needed before diving into equations of motion.

title: Newtonian Mechanics
description: Learn and master the concepts of Newtonian Mechanics with structured lessons, interactive visualizations, and practice exercises.

title: Work, Energy & Power
description: Learn and master the concepts of Work, Energy & Power with structured lessons, interactive visualizations, and practice exercises.

title: Momentum & Collisions
description: Learn and master the concepts of Momentum & Collisions with structured lessons, interactive visualizations, and practice exercises.

title: Rotational Motion
description: Learn and master the concepts of Rotational Motion with structured lessons, interactive visualizations, and practice exercises.

title: Gravitation
description: Learn and master the concepts of Gravitation with structured lessons, interactive visualizations, and practice exercises.

title: Oscillations & Simple Harmonic Motion (SHM)
description: Learn and master the concepts of Oscillations & Simple Harmonic Motion (SHM) with structured lessons, interactive visualizations, and practice exercises.

title: Waves & Sound
description: Learn and master the concepts of Waves & Sound with structured lessons, interactive visualizations, and practice exercises.

title: Thermodynamics
description: Learn and master the concepts of Thermodynamics with structured lessons, interactive visualizations, and practice exercises.

title: Fluid Mechanics
description: Learn and master the concepts of Fluid Mechanics with structured lessons, interactive visualizations, and practice exercises.

title: Electricity (Electrostatics)
description: Learn and master the concepts of Electricity (Electrostatics) with structured lessons, interactive visualizations, and practice exercises.

title: Magnetism
description: Learn and master the concepts of Magnetism with structured lessons, interactive visualizations, and practice exercises.

title: Electromagnetism
description: Here are the two missing courses in the same format as the rest of your curriculum.

title: Optics
description: At this point, you've completed the entire **Classical Physics** sequence. The remaining courses are the **Modern Physics** sequence, covering 20th-century and contemporary physics.

title: Modern Physics
description: Learn and master the concepts of Modern Physics with structured lessons, interactive visualizations, and practice exercises.

title: Electrical Circuits
description: These two courses complete your **25-course Physics curriculum** in a consistent style with the other courses.

title: Quantum Mechanics
description: This course focuses on **analyzing and designing electrical circuits**. While Electromagnetism explains *why* electricity behaves the way it does, this course teaches *how to analyze real electrical networks*.

title: Atomic & Nuclear Physics
description: At this stage, you've essentially completed the full undergraduate core of physics. The remaining courses focus on specialized domains built on quantum mechanics, electromagnetism, and relativity.

title: Solid State Physics
description: Learn and master the concepts of Solid State Physics with structured lessons, interactive visualizations, and practice exercises.

title: Particle Physics
description: Learn and master the concepts of Particle Physics with structured lessons, interactive visualizations, and practice exercises.

title: Relativity
description: You're now into the final four advanced physics courses. **Relativity** is next, covering Einstein's Special and General Relativity, spacetime, black holes, gravitational waves, and the geometry of the universe.

title: Plasma Physics
description: After this, the final three courses explore large-scale and high-energy phenomena—from ionized gases and stars to galaxies and the evolution of the entire universe.

title: Astrophysics
description: These final two courses complete the physics curriculum by moving from **stars, galaxies, and black holes** to the **origin, evolution, and fate of the universe itself**.

title: Cosmology
description: It serves as the capstone course for the entire physics curriculum.
